headline: State economic development corporations hold RM45 billion in unreported assets
context: Thirteen state economic development corporations collectively hold an estimated RM45 billion in assets but most publish no annual reports or financial statements.

cards.big: 13 state development corporations hold RM45 billion — most publish zero financial data
cards.sub: State-owned entities managing billions operate in near-complete darkness

cards.big: State economic development corporations are incorporated under state enactments
cards.sub: Each operates under different legal frameworks with varying disclosure requirements

cards.big: RM45 billion in assets includes prime land, commercial properties, and equity holdings
cards.sub: Public wealth managed without public visibility into performance or returns

cards.big: Only Selangor and Penang SEDCs publish audited annual reports consistently
cards.sub: 11 state corporations operate without regular public financial reporting

cards.big: RM45 billion in public assets without public reporting is the definition of accountability failure
Should all state development corporations follow uniform financial reporting standards?
